page_content:
{"Question": "How do I analyze a basic user conversion funnel?", "Context": "I have event data with user_id and event_name columns. I want to analyze how users move through a signup funnel with specific steps.", "Code_Example": "from chatbot.tools.mltools.funnelanalysis import CohortPipe, analyze_funnel

# Create pipeline with your event data
pipeline = CohortPipe.from_dataframe(event_df)

# Define your funnel steps
funnel_steps = ['view_product', 'add_to_cart', 'start_checkout', 'purchase']

# Optional: Define friendly names for the steps
step_names = ['Product View', 'Add to Cart', 'Start Checkout', 'Purchase']

# Analyze the funnel
funnel_results = pipeline | analyze_funnel(
    event_column='event_name',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    funnel_steps=funnel_steps,
    step_names=step_names
)

# Get the funnel summary
overall_funnel = funnel_results.conversion_funnels['overall_funnel']
print(overall_funnel)", "Usage": "analyze_funnel(event_column='event_name', user_id_column='user_id', funnel_steps=['view_product', 'add_to_cart', 'start_checkout', 'purchase'])"}

metadata.description: Analyze a basic user conversion funnel

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I analyze funnel performance over time?", "Context": "I want to see how my conversion funnel performs across different time periods (e.g., months) to identify trends.", "Code_Example": "from chatbot.tools.mltools.funnelanalysis import CohortPipe, analyze_funnel_by_time

# Create pipeline with your event data
pipeline = CohortPipe.from_dataframe(event_df)

# Define your funnel steps
funnel_steps = ['view_product', 'add_to_cart', 'start_checkout', 'purchase']

# Analyze the funnel by time periods (monthly)
time_funnel = pipeline | analyze_funnel_by_time(
    event_column='event_name',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    date_column='event_timestamp',  # Column with event dates
    funnel_steps=funnel_steps,
    time_period='M',  # 'M' for monthly
    max_periods=6  # Show last 6 months
)

# Get the time-based funnel summary
time_results = time_funnel.conversion_funnels['time_funnel']
print(time_results)

# Analyze trends in the 'purchase' conversion rate over time
purchase_trends = time_results[['cohort', 'Purchase_rate']]
print(purchase_trends)", "Usage": "analyze_funnel_by_time(event_column='event_name', user_id_column='user_id', date_column='event_timestamp', funnel_steps=funnel_steps, time_period='M', max_periods=6)"}

metadata.description: Analyze funnel performance across different time periods

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I compare funnel performance across different user segments?", "Context": "I want to analyze how different user segments (e.g., by device_type or user_country) convert through my funnel.", "Code_Example": "from chatbot.tools.mltools.funnelanalysis import CohortPipe, analyze_funnel_by_segment

# Create pipeline with your event data
pipeline = CohortPipe.from_dataframe(event_df)

# Define your funnel steps
funnel_steps = ['view_product', 'add_to_cart', 'start_checkout', 'purchase']

# Analyze the funnel by segment
segment_funnel = pipeline | analyze_funnel_by_segment(
    event_column='event_name',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    segment_column='device_type',  # Column to segment by
    funnel_steps=funnel_steps,
    min_users=20  # Minimum users per segment
)

# Get the segment-based funnel summary
segment_results = segment_funnel.conversion_funnels['segment_funnel']

# Compare purchase conversion rates across segments
purchase_by_segment = segment_results[['segment', 'purchase_rate']].sort_values('purchase_rate', ascending=False)
print(purchase_by_segment)", "Usage": "analyze_funnel_by_segment(event_column='event_name', user_id_column='user_id', segment_column='device_type', funnel_steps=funnel_steps, min_users=20)"}

metadata.description: Compare funnel performance across different user segments

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I discover the actual paths users take through my product?", "Context": "I want to understand the common sequences of events users perform, not just the ideal funnel path.", "Code_Example": "from chatbot.tools.mltools.funnelanalysis import CohortPipe, analyze_user_paths

# Create pipeline with your event data
pipeline = CohortPipe.from_dataframe(event_df)

# Define the key events you're interested in
key_events = ['homepage_view', 'search', 'view_product', 'add_to_cart', 'start_checkout', 'purchase']

# Analyze the actual paths users take
user_path_analysis = pipeline | analyze_user_paths(
    event_column='event_name',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    timestamp_column='event_timestamp',
    funnel_steps=key_events,  # All events we want to track
    max_path_length=8,  # Limit path length for readability
    min_path_frequency=10  # Only show paths taken by at least 10 users
)

# Get the common paths users take
path_results = user_path_analysis.conversion_funnels['user_paths']
common_paths = path_results['path_counts']

# Display the most common user paths
top_paths = common_paths.head(10)  # Top 10 most common paths
print(top_paths[['path', 'frequency', 'percentage']])", "Usage": "analyze_user_paths(event_column='event_name', user_id_column='user_id', timestamp_column='event_timestamp', funnel_steps=key_events, max_path_length=8, min_path_frequency=10)"}

metadata.description: Discover common paths users take through your product

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I retrieve the results of my funnel analysis?", "Context": "I've run multiple funnel analyses and want to access the results for further processing or visualization.", "Code_Example": "from chatbot.tools.mltools.funnelanalysis import CohortPipe, analyze_funnel, analyze_funnel_by_time, get_funnel_summary

# Create pipeline with your event data
pipeline = CohortPipe.from_dataframe(event_df)

# Define your funnel steps
funnel_steps = ['view_product', 'add_to_cart', 'start_checkout', 'purchase']

# Run multiple funnel analyses
funnel_pipeline = (pipeline
    | analyze_funnel(event_column='event_name', user_id_column='user_id', funnel_steps=funnel_steps)
    | analyze_funnel_by_time(event_column='event_name', user_id_column='user_id', date_column='event_timestamp', funnel_steps=funnel_steps)
)

# Retrieve all funnel summaries
all_funnel_data = funnel_pipeline | get_funnel_summary()

# Access specific funnel results
overall_funnel = all_funnel_data['overall_funnel']
time_funnel = all_funnel_data['time_funnel']

# Now you can use these results for visualization or export
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Plot overall funnel
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.bar(overall_funnel['step'], overall_funnel['count'])
plt.title('Conversion Funnel')
plt.ylabel('Number of Users')
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()", "Usage": "get_funnel_summary()"}

metadata.description: Retrieve funnel analysis results for further processing

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I compare different segments against a baseline for a specific funnel metric?", "Context": "I want to see how different user segments perform compared to a baseline segment or the average for a specific conversion rate.", "Code_Example": "from chatbot.tools.mltools.funnelanalysis import CohortPipe, analyze_funnel_by_segment, compare_segments

# Create pipeline with your event data
pipeline = CohortPipe.from_dataframe(event_df)

# Define your funnel steps
funnel_steps = ['view_product', 'add_to_cart', 'start_checkout', 'purchase']

# First run segment analysis
segment_funnel = pipeline | analyze_funnel_by_segment(
    event_column='event_name',
    user_id_column='user_id',
    segment_column='country',  # Segment by country
    funnel_steps=funnel_steps
)

# Compare purchase rate across countries against the US as baseline
comparison_results = segment_funnel | compare_segments(
    metric_column='purchase_rate',  # The metric to compare
    baseline_segment='US'  # Use US as the baseline
)

# Display comparison results
print(comparison_results[['segment', 'purchase_rate', 'baseline', 'abs_diff', 'rel_diff']])

# For comparison against the average (no baseline specified)
avg_comparison = segment_funnel | compare_segments(
    metric_column='purchase_rate'  # Compare against average
)

print(avg_comparison[['segment', 'purchase_rate', 'baseline', 'rel_diff']])", "Usage": "compare_segments(metric_column='purchase_rate', baseline_segment='US')"}

metadata.description: Compare segments against a baseline for funnel metrics
